pages.title: Contact | Sir Mark Hendrick MP
pages.text_excerpt: --> Contact | Sir Mark Hendrick MP top of page Sir Mark Hendrick Labour and Co-operative MP for Preston Home Contact About News Newsletters Parliament Written Questions Media Gallery More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. mark.hendrick.mp@parliament.uk 01772 883575 Contact Please check that you are one of my constituents before you contact me by entering your postcode here . ​ ​ If your issue relates to Preston City Council or Lancashire County Council, then please approach your Council representative. You can find your representative by clicking the links below: ​ Preston City Councillor ​ Lancashire County Councillor ​ If your issue relates to parliamentary or national issues, please feel free to contact me. Please explain the issue as clearly as possible and include all relevant information, including : your full name postal address including postcode contact telephone number the name of the organisation that you are having issues with reference numbers (if applicable) relevant dates further names, addresses and dates of birth of other affected individuals (if relevant) ​ If your email contains sensitive information that you do not wish to be disclosed to a third party, please indicate this clearly in your email. ​ Please email me at mark.hendrick.mp@parliament.uk ​ If you would prefer to write to me, my correspondence address is: ​ Sir Mark Hendrick MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA ​ ​ ©2025 by Mark Hendrick MP Privacy Notice bottom of page

pages.title: About | Mark Hendrick
pages.text_excerpt: --> About | Mark Hendrick top of page Sir Mark Hendrick Labour and Co-operative MP for Preston Home Contact About News Newsletters Parliament Written Questions Media Gallery More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. mark.hendrick.mp@parliament.uk 01772 883575 About A former electronics engineer and college lecturer, Sir Mark began his political career as a Salford City Councillor in 1987, before being elected as a Member of the European Parliament for Central Lancashire in 1994. ​ Sir Mark became MP for Preston at the November 2000 by-election and has been re-elected at every General Election since. He has been on several influential parliamentary committees including the Foreign Affairs Select Committee, International Development Select Committee, European Scrutiny Select Committee and the International Trade Select Committee. He is also serves on Mr Speaker's Panel of Chairs and is currently the Chair of the APPG for Germany, Japan and Vietnam. ​ Sir Mark was awarded a Knighthood for services to Parliamentary and Political Service in the Queen’s New Year’s Honours List 2018. Get in Touch ©2025 by Mark Hendrick MP Privacy Notice bottom of page

headings: h2: Prizes for 'egg'ceptional Easter Egg competition winners at Deepdale Primary School
h2: Over £900 raised by MP staff team for the Big PNE Sleep-out!
h2: Official Opening of Lumo HQ in Preston
pages.text_excerpt: --> News | Sir Mark Hendrick MP top of page Sir Mark Hendrick Labour and Co-operative MP for Preston Home Contact About News Newsletters Parliament Written Questions Media Gallery More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. mark.hendrick.mp@parliament.uk 01772 883575 All Posts Search Prizes for 'egg'ceptional Easter Egg competition winners at Deepdale Primary School I was delighted to be invited to the Family Easter Egg Assembly at Deepdale Primary School, #Preston to present prizes to Year 5 and 6 winners and their families. Pictured with Headteacher Miss Mirza and Year 6 Senior Teacher, Mrs Woodward. The entries were very creative and imaginative and it’s great to hear that the competition is open to pupils and their families to create a 2D or 3D entry. The entries were ‘egg’ceptional and this annual event has become a much-loved part Mar 27 Over £900 raised by MP staff team for the Big PNE Sleep-out! Well done to everyone who took part in the The Big PNE Sleep Out over the weekend, including members of my staff team who took part for the first time this year. Homelessness is still a concerning issue locally and official statistics from Autumn 2025 indicates that the largest regional increase was in the North West, rising from 367 in 2024 to 441 in 2025 - an increase of 20%. In recent months, I have led campaigns about tackling Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG), so i Mar 21 Eid Mubarak Wishing all Muslims in #Preston and all my Muslim friends and colleagues, a very Happy Eid. I hope you have a joyous day full of peace and happiness celebrating with loved ones, after a month of fasting, reflection and prayer. #EidulFitr2026 #EidMubarak #Eid2026 Mar 20 Official Opening of Lumo HQ in Preston I’m delighted and honoured to officially open the new Lumo Headquarters in #Preston, ahead of their frequent and low-cost rail services between London and Stirling in Scotland. Pictured with Stuart Jones, Managing Director, First Rail Open Access This is a very exciting development for our City. The new rail hub will bring jobs for local people, quality apprenticeships, low-cost rail fares, more route options and better connectivity between London, Scotland and other key Wet Mar 20 ©2025 by Mark Hendrick MP Privacy Notice bottom of page
